Study 3 - Celiktutan, Klesse & Tuk (2024)

Load packages

library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(lme4)
library(emmeans)

Load pretest data

pretest <- read_excel("Data_Study3_pretest_osf.xlsx")
pretest <- pretest[pretest$Progress == 100, ]

Pretest sample size

nrow(pretest)
## [1] 83

Pretest gender

table(pretest$gender)
## 
##  1  2 
## 40 43
prop.table(table(pretest$gender))
## 
##         1         2 
## 0.4819277 0.5180723

Pretest age

summary(pretest$age)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##   18.00   19.00   19.00   19.34   20.00   22.00

Pretest behavior categorization

behavior_cols <- c("B1", "B2", "B3", "B4", "B5", "B6", "B7", "B8", "B9", "B10")

for (col in behavior_cols) {
  cat(col, "\n")
  print(round(prop.table(table(pretest[[col]])) * 100, 2))
}
## B1 
## 
##     1     2 
## 91.57  8.43 
## B2 
## 
##     1     2 
## 42.17 57.83 
## B3 
## 
##     1     2 
## 79.52 20.48 
## B4 
## 
##     1     2 
## 31.33 68.67 
## B5 
## 
##     1     2 
## 66.27 33.73 
## B6 
## 
##     1     2 
## 78.31 21.69 
## B7 
## 
##     1     2 
## 18.07 81.93 
## B8 
## 
##     1     2 
##  9.64 90.36 
## B9 
## 
##     1     2 
##  9.64 90.36 
## B10 
## 
##     1     2 
## 21.69 78.31

Pretest outsourcing percentages

outsource_pct <- data.frame(
  Behavior = 1:10,
  outsource = c(8.43, 57.83, 20.48, 68.67, 33.73, 21.69, 81.93, 90.36, 90.36, 78.31)
)

outsource_pct
##    Behavior outsource
## 1         1      8.43
## 2         2     57.83
## 3         3     20.48
## 4         4     68.67
## 5         5     33.73
## 6         6     21.69
## 7         7     81.93
## 8         8     90.36
## 9         9     90.36
## 10       10     78.31

Pretest outsourcing bar chart

ggplot(outsource_pct, aes(x = factor(Behavior), y = outsource)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  labs(x = "Behavior", y = "Outsourcing (%)", title = "Pretest: Behaviors seen as outsourcing") +
  theme_minimal()

Load main study data

dat <- read_excel("Data_Study3_osf.xlsx")
dat <- dat[dat$Status == 0, ]
dat <- dat[dat$Progress == 100, ]

Main study sample size

nrow(dat)
## [1] 263

Main study gender

table(dat$gender)
## 
##   1   2 
## 140 123
prop.table(table(dat$gender))
## 
##         1         2 
## 0.5323194 0.4676806

Main study age

summary(dat$age)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##   18.00   19.00   19.00   19.15   20.00   25.00

Remove missing responses

dat <- dat[
  dat$self != 1 |
    (!is.na(dat$B1_self) & !is.na(dat$B2_self) & !is.na(dat$B3_self) &
     !is.na(dat$B4_self) & !is.na(dat$B5_self) & !is.na(dat$B6_self) &
     !is.na(dat$B7_self) & !is.na(dat$B8_self) & !is.na(dat$B9_self) &
     !is.na(dat$B10_self)),
]

dat <- dat[
  dat$self != 0 |
    (!is.na(dat$B1_other) & !is.na(dat$B2_other) & !is.na(dat$B3_other) &
     !is.na(dat$B4_other) & !is.na(dat$B5_other) & !is.na(dat$B6_other) &
     !is.na(dat$B7_other) & !is.na(dat$B8_other) & !is.na(dat$B9_other) &
     !is.na(dat$B10_other)),
]

Participants per condition

table(dat$self)
## 
##   0   1 
## 124 126
prop.table(table(dat$self))
## 
##     0     1 
## 0.496 0.504

Condition bar chart

cond_table <- as.data.frame(table(dat$self))
names(cond_table) <- c("Condition", "Count")
cond_table$Condition <- ifelse(cond_table$Condition == 1, "Self", "Other")

ggplot(cond_table, aes(x = Condition, y = Count)) +
  geom_col(fill = "darkorange") +
  labs(title = "Participants per condition") +
  theme_minimal()

Create usage likelihood variables

dat$B1 <- ifelse(dat$self == 1, dat$B1_self, dat$B1_other)
dat$B2 <- ifelse(dat$self == 1, dat$B2_self, dat$B2_other)
dat$B3 <- ifelse(dat$self == 1, dat$B3_self, dat$B3_other)
dat$B4 <- ifelse(dat$self == 1, dat$B4_self, dat$B4_other)
dat$B5 <- ifelse(dat$self == 1, dat$B5_self, dat$B5_other)
dat$B6 <- ifelse(dat$self == 1, dat$B6_self, dat$B6_other)
dat$B7 <- ifelse(dat$self == 1, dat$B7_self, dat$B7_other)
dat$B8 <- ifelse(dat$self == 1, dat$B8_self, dat$B8_other)
dat$B9 <- ifelse(dat$self == 1, dat$B9_self, dat$B9_other)
dat$B10 <- ifelse(dat$self == 1, dat$B10_self, dat$B10_other)

Reshape data to long format

dat_long <- dat %>%
  select(ResponseId, self, B1, B2, B3, B4, B5, B6, B7, B8, B9, B10) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("B"), names_to = "Behavior", values_to = "Likelihood")

dat_long$Behavior <- as.numeric(gsub("B", "", dat_long$Behavior))

Add outsourcing score from pretest

dat_long$outsource <- outsource_pct$outsource[match(dat_long$Behavior, outsource_pct$Behavior)]
dat_long$outsource_centered <- dat_long$outsource - mean(dat_long$outsource)

Descriptive statistics

summary(dat_long$Likelihood)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##   1.000   3.000   4.000   4.164   6.000   7.000
summary(dat_long$outsource_centered)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
## -46.749 -33.489   8.071   0.000  26.751  35.181

Mean likelihood by condition

dat_long %>%
  group_by(self) %>%
  summarise(mean_likelihood = mean(Likelihood))
## # A tibble: 2 × 2
##   self  mean_likelihood
##   <chr>           <dbl>
## 1 0                4.69
## 2 1                3.65

Mean likelihood by behavior

dat_long %>%
  group_by(Behavior) %>%
  summarise(mean_likelihood = mean(Likelihood))
## # A tibble: 10 × 2
##    Behavior mean_likelihood
##       <dbl>           <dbl>
##  1        1            5.26
##  2        2            4.52
##  3        3            4.57
##  4        4            4.52
##  5        5            4.02
##  6        6            4.50
##  7        7            3.28
##  8        8            4.58
##  9        9            3.83
## 10       10            2.54

Mean likelihood by behavior and condition

means_plot <- dat_long %>%
  group_by(Behavior, self) %>%
  summarise(mean_likelihood = mean(Likelihood), .groups = "drop")

means_plot$Condition <- ifelse(means_plot$self == 1, "Self", "Other")

Bar chart of mean likelihood by behavior

ggplot(means_plot, aes(x = factor(Behavior), y = mean_likelihood, fill = Condition)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  labs(x = "Behavior", y = "Mean likelihood", title = "Usage likelihood by behavior") +
  theme_minimal()

Main analysis: mixed effects model

mixedreg <- lmer(Likelihood ~ self * outsource_centered + (1 | ResponseId), data = dat_long)
summary(mixedreg)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod']
## Formula: Likelihood ~ self * outsource_centered + (1 | ResponseId)
##    Data: dat_long
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 9538.6
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -2.91326 -0.68481  0.07034  0.69310  2.75030 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups     Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  ResponseId (Intercept) 0.6702   0.8187  
##  Residual               2.2959   1.5152  
## Number of obs: 2500, groups:  ResponseId, 250
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                           Estimate Std. Error t value
## (Intercept)               4.687097   0.085186  55.022
## self1                    -1.038684   0.119991  -8.656
## outsource_centered       -0.010322   0.001443  -7.151
## self1:outsource_centered -0.007604   0.002033  -3.740
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) self1  otsrc_
## self1       -0.710              
## otsrc_cntrd  0.000  0.000       
## slf1:tsrc_c  0.000  0.000 -0.710

Planned contrasts

probe <- emmeans(mixedreg, ~ self * outsource_centered,
                 at = list(outsource_centered = c(-40, -30, -20, -10, 0, 10, 20, 30)))
contrast(probe, "revpairwise", by = "outsource_centered")
## outsource_centered = -40:
##  contrast      estimate    SE  df t.ratio p.value
##  self1 - self0   -0.735 0.145 516  -5.067 <0.0001
## 
## outsource_centered = -30:
##  contrast      estimate    SE  df t.ratio p.value
##  self1 - self0   -0.811 0.135 390  -6.022 <0.0001
## 
## outsource_centered = -20:
##  contrast      estimate    SE  df t.ratio p.value
##  self1 - self0   -0.887 0.127 308  -6.998 <0.0001
## 
## outsource_centered = -10:
##  contrast      estimate    SE  df t.ratio p.value
##  self1 - self0   -0.963 0.122 262  -7.910 <0.0001
## 
## outsource_centered =   0:
##  contrast      estimate    SE  df t.ratio p.value
##  self1 - self0   -1.039 0.120 248  -8.656 <0.0001
## 
## outsource_centered =  10:
##  contrast      estimate    SE  df t.ratio p.value
##  self1 - self0   -1.115 0.122 262  -9.159 <0.0001
## 
## outsource_centered =  20:
##  contrast      estimate    SE  df t.ratio p.value
##  self1 - self0   -1.191 0.127 308  -9.399 <0.0001
## 
## outsource_centered =  30:
##  contrast      estimate    SE  df t.ratio p.value
##  self1 - self0   -1.267 0.135 390  -9.411 <0.0001
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger

Prepare data for interaction plot

dat_long$Condition <- ifelse(dat_long$self == 1, "Self", "Other")
plot_model <- lm(Likelihood ~ Condition * outsource_centered, data = dat_long)

Interaction plot data

plotdat <- expand.grid(
  Condition = c("Self", "Other"),
  outsource_centered = seq(min(dat_long$outsource_centered), max(dat_long$outsource_centered), length.out = 100)
)

plotdat$Likelihood <- predict(plot_model, newdata = plotdat)

Interaction plot

ggplot(plotdat, aes(x = outsource_centered, y = Likelihood, color = Condition)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  labs(
    x = "Outsourcing (centered)",
    y = "Usage likelihood",
    title = "Self-other difference in usage likelihood"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Self" = "coral", "Other" = "steelblue")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

Boxplot of likelihood by condition

ggplot(dat_long, aes(x = Condition, y = Likelihood, fill = Condition)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Usage likelihood: self vs other") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Self" = "coral", "Other" = "steelblue")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
